title: Day 08 Interview Prep
description: Practice useEffect lifecycle, fetch vs Axios, loading strategies, and error boundary concepts from today's roadmap.

questions.question: What is the useEffect lifecycle in functional components?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Basic understanding of when effects run relative to render.
keyPoints: Cleanup runs before next effect or unmount
Not the same as class component lifecycle names but analogous
questions.sampleAnswer: useEffect schedules work after React commits the render to the DOM. The setup function runs when the component mounts and when dependencies change. If I return a cleanup function, it runs before the next effect execution and on unmount. I use it to sync with APIs, subscriptions, or timers.
followUps: What is the difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect?

questions.question: Fetch vs Axios — when do you use each?
keyPoints: Axios offers interceptors, transforms, wider defaults
questions.sampleAnswer: I default to fetch for simple JSON APIs in Vite/React — no install, tree-shaking friendly. Axios makes sense when I need request/response interceptors for auth tokens across many endpoints, automatic JSON transforms, or cancel tokens at scale. I pick based on project complexity and team conventions.
followUps: How do you attach auth headers with fetch?

questions.question: What loading strategies do you use in data-fetching UIs?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: UX awareness beyond a single spinner.
keyPoints: Avoid showing stale data during refetch
questions.sampleAnswer: For dashboards I use skeletons that match final card layout to reduce layout shift. I disable the search button during fetch. I clear or dim stale data when a new query starts so users do not misread old results. Spinners alone are fine for small actions but skeletons scale better for content-heavy views.

questions.question: Explain the useEffect dependency array. What happens with [] vs no array vs [query]?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Can they prevent infinite loop bugs?
keyPoints: Stable references required for object deps
questions.sampleAnswer: Omitting the array runs the effect after every render — dangerous with setState inside. Empty array runs once on mount, useful for initial data if appropriate. [query] re-runs when query changes — right for refetch-on-search. Dependencies must be stable; inline objects cause repeated runs.

questions.question: How do you type a nested API response in TypeScript?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Real-world API modeling, not trivial types.
keyPoints: Separate API types from UI view models if shapes differ
questions.sampleAnswer: I define interfaces matching API JSON — WeatherResponse with nested MainBlock and WeatherCondition array. I use optional ? for fields that may be absent. If I transform API data for UI, I map to a separate WeatherViewModel so components do not depend on raw API quirks.
followUps: Zod vs interfaces for API validation?

questions.question: What is an Error Boundary and how is it different from try/catch around fetch?
keyPoints: Error boundaries catch render/lifecycle errors in children
try/catch handles async errors in effects/handlers
react-error-boundary package common in modern apps
questions.sampleAnswer: Error boundaries catch JavaScript errors during rendering in child components and show fallback UI — they do not catch async errors inside useEffect unless you rethrow into render. try/catch around fetch handles network failures. Production apps use both: try/catch for fetch errors, error boundary for unexpected render crashes.
followUps: Can functional components be error boundaries natively?

questions.question: Your weather app fires two requests when the user double-clicks Search. How do you fix it?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Race conditions and UX edge cases.
keyPoints: Ignore stale responses with cleanup flag or AbortController
Debounce is Day 09 — mention but not required today
questions.sampleAnswer: Immediate fixes: disable submit while isLoading, and use an AbortController or cancelled boolean in useEffect cleanup so only the latest response updates state. For double-click specifically, disabling the button is the fastest win. AbortController.abort() on cleanup is the robust pattern when query changes mid-flight.
followUps: How does AbortController work with fetch?

questions.question: Network succeeds but weather.weather[0] crashes the app. What failed and how do you harden?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Defensive coding with typed optional data.
keyPoints: Validate shape after parse or use type guards
questions.sampleAnswer: I assumed weather array always has elements. API can return unexpected shapes. I add optional chaining weather?.[0]?.description, validate response with a type guard or Zod after JSON parse, and show 'Data unavailable' instead of crashing. Error boundaries are last resort — validate at the boundary.

questions.question: A teammate fetches weather in the component body on every render instead of useEffect. Convince them to refactor — what do you say?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Code review communication and React rules.
keyPoints: Render must stay pure — no side effects during render
useEffect or event handler is correct home
Mentor tone with concrete bug demonstration
questions.sampleAnswer: I show Network tab: requests fire every render, including re-renders from unrelated state. I explain render should be a pure function of props and state. Side effects belong in useEffect with proper deps or in event handlers. I pair on moving fetch to submit handler or useEffect with [query], add a test city search, and document the rule in PR comment — not lecture.
redFlags: Side effects in render are fine if it works
Cannot explain why render must be pure

questions.question: How would you structure the weather feature folder for a growing React app?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Scalability thinking from day one of API features.
keyPoints: api or services layer for fetch
hooks folder ready for Day 09 extraction
questions.sampleAnswer: features/weather/ with components WeatherCard, WeatherSkeleton, SearchForm; api/fetchWeather.ts for HTTP; types/weather.ts for interfaces; WeatherPage.tsx composes them. When search logic grows, I extract useWeather(query) on Day 09. Keeps fetch out of JSX and types out of components.
